title: Who’s News: Strategic Appointments at Photonic, Aliro Quantum, Florida Atlantic University, QuSecure, Quantum Elements, and QAI Ventures - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Quantum-sector personnel moves this week span commercial, academic, government-security, and venture organizations, several tied to recent funding rounds, hardware deployments, or federal post-quantum cryptography mandates.

insights.claim: Photonic Inc. appointed Orlagh Neary (formerly General Manager of Microsoft Quantum and AI Ecosystem Engagement) as Chief Marketing Officer and Briony Shipman (formerly head of Cyber and Physical Defence, Data Security and Supply Chain at Lloyds Banking Group, and a 14-year GCHQ veteran) as VP Global Government Affairs, immediately following the close of Photonic's $200 million funding round in May 2026.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Fresh $200M capital plus a dedicated government-affairs hire signals Photonic is positioning for deeper government and regulatory engagement as it moves toward next-stage commercialization.
insights.quote: The executive hires follow the close of Photonic's $200 million USD funding round in May 2026.

insights.claim: QuSecure appointed retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral Doug Small, former Commander of Naval Information Warfare Systems Command (NAVWAR, roughly a $6 billion annual budget) and first Direct Reporting Program Manager for Project Overmatch, to its Federal Advisory Board to help the company navigate federal post-quantum cryptography migration mandates, including recent Department of War directives.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals QuSecure is positioning to win federal PQC-migration contracts amid new government mandates, a near-term government-spending catalyst to monitor.
insights.quote: In his advisory capacity, Small will assist QuSecure in addressing federal post-quantum cryptography (PQC) migration mandates, including recent directives from the Department of War.

insights.claim: Aliro Quantum named Mohiuddin (Moe) Muneeb Khan as Senior Vice President of Engineering to run software engineering as the company scales hardware-interoperable platforms for quantum secure communications and quantum position verification applications.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Quantum position verification is an emerging defense-adjacent application area now drawing dedicated executive investment, worth tracking as a growth vector.
insights.quote: he will manage the company's software engineering teams as Aliro scales its hardware-interoperable platforms for quantum secure communications and quantum position verification applications

insights.claim: Florida Atlantic University appointed Robert Loredo (20+ years in quantum workforce development, holder of more than 270 patents) as Executive Director of its Center for Quantum Technologies effective July 1, 2026, anchoring the university's upcoming on-campus deployment of a D-Wave Advantage2 annealing quantum system.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Another academic D-Wave Advantage2 deployment expands D-Wave's installed base and signals continued academic demand for annealing hardware.
insights.quote: anchoring the upcoming on-campus deployment of a D-Wave Advantage2 annealing quantum system

insights.claim: Quantum Elements named Ben Pressley (former CRO/Advisor at fabric, EVP of Sales, Operations & Strategy at Afterpay, and VP of Worldwide Sales at Magento) as Chief Revenue Officer to lead global revenue strategy for its digital twin software platforms.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A commercially seasoned, non-quantum-native CRO hire suggests Quantum Elements is pushing toward go-to-market maturity for its digital-twin product line.
insights.quote: Pressley will manage the company's global revenue strategy, commercial operations, and customer growth frameworks for its digital twin software platforms

insights.claim: QAI Ventures is transitioning its Singapore leadership: Lisa Schroeder becomes Managing Director, Singapore, taking over APAC operations from Thomas Landis, who built the firm's original APAC presence and will return to Switzerland and exit the organization.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Leadership continuity change at a quantum-focused venture firm's APAC arm is worth monitoring for any near-term shift in APAC quantum investment activity.
insights.quote: Landis, who previously contributed to the firm's operational frameworks in Switzerland before expanding the Singapore office, will return to Switzerland and transition out of the organization.
